messages.content: I have a calendar on web page. First, I need to select data-range-key=“пользовательский”. Calendar consists of two parts. The left part is used to select the start date. It is placed in class="drp-calendar left". The right part is used to select the end date. It is placed in class="drp-calendar right". In both parts value="0" in class="monthselect" that is class="month".

To select the start date in the left part of the calendar, you can use the following code:

```python
start_calendar = driver.find_element_by_css_selector('[data-range-key="пользовательский"] .drp-calendar.left')
start_month_select = start_calendar.find_element_by_css_selector('.monthselect .month')
start_month_select.click()
```

Similarly, to select the end date in the right part of the calendar, you can use the following code:

```python
end_calendar = driver.find_element_by_css_selector('[data-range-key="пользовательский"] .drp-calendar.right')
end_month_select = end_calendar.find_element_by_css_selector('.monthselect .month')
end_month_select.click()
```

Make sure to replace `driver` with the appropriate WebDriver instance you are using in your code.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
AttributeError                            Traceback (most recent call last)
Cell In[85], line 1
----> 1 start_calendar = driver.find_element_by_css_selector('[data-range-key="пользовательский"] .drp-calendar.left')
      2 start_month_select = start_calendar.find_element_by_css_selector('.monthselect .month')
      3 start_month_select.click()

AttributeError: 'WebDriver' object has no attribute 'find_element_by_css_selector'

I apologize for the confusion. It seems that the WebDriver object you're using does not have the `find_element_by_css_selector` method. Please make sure you are using the correct WebDriver.

Alternatively, you can try using the `find_element` method with other locators like `By.CSS_SELECTOR` or `By.XPATH`. Here's an example using the `find_element` method with CSS selector:

```python
from selenium.webdriver.common.by import By

start_calendar = driver.find_element(By.CSS_SELECTOR, '[data-range-key="пользовательский"] .drp-calendar.left')
start_month_select = start_calendar.find_element(By.CSS_SELECTOR, '.monthselect .month')
start_month_select.click()
```

Again, ensure that you have the correct WebDriver instantiated and imported.

There is class "month" inside class "drp-calendar left". There are classes "monthselect" and  "yearselect" inside class "month". I need to select option value for "monthselect" and "yearselect". Write selenium python script

Certainly! Here's a Selenium Python script that selects the desired option values for both `monthselect` and `yearselect`:

```python
from selenium import webdriver
from selenium.webdriver.support.select import Select

# Instantiate the WebDriver (replace with the appropriate WebDriver)
driver = webdriver.Chrome()

# Open the webpage with the calendar
driver.get("https://your-webpage-url")

# Find the left part of the calendar and select the option values for monthselect and yearselect
start_calendar = driver.find_element_by_css_selector('.drp-calendar.left')
month_select = Select(start_calendar.find_element_by_css_selector('.monthselect'))
year_select = Select(start_calendar.find_element_by_css_selector('.yearselect'))

# Select the desired option value for monthselect
month_select.select_by_value("2")  # Replace "2" with the desired option value

# Select the desired option value for yearselect
year_select.select_by_value("2022")  # Replace "2022" with the desired option value

# Close the browser
driver.quit()
```

Make sure to replace `https://your-webpage-url` with the actual URL of the webpage containing the calendar. Also, replace the option values (`2` and `2022`) with the values you want to select in `monthselect` and `yearselect`, respectively.

AttributeError: 'WebDriver' object has no attribute 'find_element_by_css_selector'

Apologies for the mistake. The error occurred because the WebDriver object does not have the `find_element_by_css_selector` method. Please make sure you have imported the necessary modules correctly, including `webdriver` and `Select` from `selenium.webdriver.support.select`.

To fix the issue, modify the code as follows:

```python
from selenium import webdriver
from selenium.webdriver.support.select import Select

# Instantiate the WebDriver (replace with the appropriate WebDriver)
driver = webdriver.Chrome()

# Open the webpage with the calendar
driver.get("https://your-webpage-url")

# Find the left part of the calendar and select the option values for monthselect and yearselect
start_calendar = driver.find_element(By.CSS_SELECTOR, '.drp-calendar.left')
month_select = Select(start_calendar.find_element(By.CSS_SELECTOR, '.monthselect'))
year_select = Select(start_calendar.find_element(By.CSS_SELECTOR, '.yearselect'))

# Select the desired option value for monthselect
month_select.select_by_value("2")  # Replace "2" with the desired option value

# Select the desired option value for yearselect
year_select.select_by_value("2022")  # Replace "2022" with the desired option value

# Close the browser
driver.quit()
```

Make sure to replace `https://your-webpage-url` with the actual URL of the webpage containing the calendar. Also, replace the option values (`2` and `2022`) with the values you want to select in `monthselect` and `yearselect`, respectively.

StaleElementReferenceException: Message: stale element reference: stale element not found
  (Session info: chrome=119.0.6045.160); For documentation on this error, please visit: https://www.selenium.dev/documentation/webdriver/troubleshooting/errors#stale-element-reference-exception

The `StaleElementReferenceException` occurs when the element that you initially found becomes stale, usually due to a page refresh or change. To fix this issue, you can re-find the `yearselect` element after selecting a value in the `monthselect` element. Here's an updated version of the script that should work without causing the `StaleElementReferenceException`:

```python
from selenium import webdriver
from selenium.webdriver.support.select import Select

# Instantiate the WebDriver (replace with the appropriate WebDriver)
driver = webdriver.Chrome()

# Open the webpage with the calendar
driver.get("https://your-webpage-url")

# Find the left part of the calendar
start_calendar = driver.find_element(By.CSS_SELECTOR, '.drp-calendar.left')

# Select the desired option value for monthselect
month_select = Select(start_calendar.find_element(By.CSS_SELECTOR, '.monthselect'))
month_select.select_by_value("2")  # Replace "2" with the desired option value

# Re-find the start calendar element to avoid StaleElementReferenceException
start_calendar = driver.find_element(By.CSS_SELECTOR, '.drp-calendar.left')

# Find the yearselect element within start_calendar and select the desired option value
year_select = Select(start_calendar.find_element(By.CSS_SELECTOR, '.yearselect'))
year_select.select_by_value("2022")  # Replace "2022" with the desired option value

# Close the browser
driver.quit()
```

Remember to replace `https://your-webpage-url` with the actual URL of the webpage containing the calendar, and adjust the option values (`2` and `2022`) as needed.

Perfect! The next step is to select a day. The problem is that each day is placed in a separate class. And 1st day of each month may belong to different class. How to specify the day without refering to its class?

If each day is placed in a separate class and the class name may vary, you can use XPath to locate the day element based on its text value. Here's an example of how you can select a specific day without referring to its class:

```python
# Find the calendar element containing the days
calendar = driver.find_element(By.CSS_SELECTOR, '.drp-calendar')

# Find the day element using XPath based on its text value
desired_day = calendar.find_element(By.XPATH, f".//td[contains(text(), '1')]")

# Click on the desired day
desired_day.click()
```

In this example, `.drp-calendar` is the CSS selector for the calendar element that contains the days. The XPath expression `".//td[contains(text(), '1')]"` searches for a `td` element that contains the text '1' (replace '1' with the desired day).

After locating the desired day, you can perform actions like clicking on it using the `click()` method or any other action based on your requirements.

Continue your previous code

# Find the left part of the calendar
start_calendar = driver.find_element(By.CSS_SELECTOR, '.drp-calendar.left')

# Select the desired option value for monthselect
month_select = Select(start_calendar.find_element(By.CSS_SELECTOR, '.monthselect'))
month_select.select_by_value("10") 

# Re-find the start calendar element to avoid StaleElementReferenceException
start_calendar = driver.find_element(By.CSS_SELECTOR, '.drp-calendar.left')

# Find the yearselect element within start_calendar and select the desired option value
year_select = Select(start_calendar.find_element(By.CSS_SELECTOR, '.yearselect'))
year_select.select_by_value("1995")

Certainly! To continue from the previous code:

```python
# Find the left part of the calendar
start_calendar = driver.find_element(By.CSS_SELECTOR, '.drp-calendar.left')

# Select the desired option value for monthselect
month_select = Select(start_calendar.find_element(By.CSS_SELECTOR, '.monthselect'))
month_select.select_by_value("10")

# Re-find the start calendar element to avoid StaleElementReferenceException
start_calendar = driver.find_element(By.CSS_SELECTOR, '.drp-calendar.left')

# Find the yearselect element within start_calendar and select the desired option value
year_select = Select(start_calendar.find_element(By.CSS_SELECTOR, '.yearselect'))
year_select.select_by_value("1995")

# Find the calendar element containing the days
calendar = driver.find_element(By.CSS_SELECTOR, '.drp-calendar')

# Find the day element using XPath based on its text value
desired_day = calendar.find_element(By.XPATH, ".//td[contains(text(), '1')]")

# Click on the desired day
desired_day.click()

# Close the browser
driver.quit()
```

Make sure to replace `'10'` and `'1995'` with the desired option values for `monthselect` and `yearselect` respectively, and adjust the XPath expression based on the specific text value of the day you want to select.

The left part of calendar contains some days from previous months, all days of selected month and some days of future month. So, there can be for example two numbers 1. How to select a day in this case?

If the left part of the calendar contains days from previous months and future months, you can modify the XPath expression to select the desired day more accurately. One way to do this is by using the `ancestor` axis in XPath to ensure that the desired day is within the selected month.

Here's an example of how you can select a day in this scenario:

```python
# Find the calendar element containing the days
calendar = driver.find_element(By.CSS_SELECTOR, '.drp-calendar.left')

# Find the day element using XPath based on its text value and month context
desired_day = calendar.find_element(By.XPATH, ".//td[text()='1' and ancestor::div[contains(@class, 'month')]]")

# Click on the desired day
desired_day.click()
```

In this example, `".//td[text()='1' and ancestor::div[contains(@class, 'month')]]"` is the XPath expression. It finds a `td` element with the text '1' where its ancestor `div` contains the class 'month'. This ensures that the day belongs to the selected month while ignoring days from previous or future months.

Make sure to adjust the text value ('1' in this example) to match the specific day you want to select.

NoSuchElementException: Message: no such element: Unable to locate element: {"method":"xpath","selector":".//td[text()='1' and ancestor::div[contains(@class, 'month')]]"}
  (Session info: chrome=119.0.6045.160); For documentation on this error, please visit: https://www.selenium.dev/documentation/webdriver/troubleshooting/errors#no-such-element-exception
Stacktrace:

If the previous method didn't work as expected and you're still facing the `NoSuchElementException`, there might be a different structure or class name within your calendar. 

To handle this situation, you can try a different XPath expression that selects the desired day by searching for a `td` element with the text '1' within the selected month context. 

Here's an updated example:

```python
# Find the calendar element containing the days
calendar = driver.find_element(By.CSS_SELECTOR, '.drp-calendar.left')

# Find the day element using XPath based on its text value and month context
desired_day = calendar.find_element(By.XPATH, ".//div[contains(@class, 'month')]//td[text()='1']")

# Click on the desired day
desired_day.click()
```

In this case, we're searching for a `div` element with the class 'month', and then finding a `td` element with the text '1' within that month context.

Please ensure that you customize the class name or structure in the XPath expression if it differs in your specific calendar implementation.
